analysis: Looking at the full week, you went to bed before 9pm on both Monday and Tuesday nights — that is genuinely impressive, and on Monday you were asleep by 8:55pm which is basically on target. The deep sleep you're getting in those early evening sessions is real and it's doing work: you're stacking that slow-wave sleep right when your body wants it most. The gap worth naming is what happens in the second half of the night — particularly that 43-minute wake at 2:30am on Tuesday — where sleep gets fragmented and REM comes in scattered little bursts instead of long stretches. Nothing to spiral about, just the pattern to watch as all those interventions keep stacking up and doing their thing.
